# Implementation Plan: Add Confirmation Step to Critical Actions

**Branch**: `035-confirm-critical-actions` | **Date**: 2026-03-11 | **Spec**: `/specs/035-confirm-critical-actions/spec.md`
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/035-confirm-critical-actions/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Standardize and harden the existing confirmation infrastructure across all critical/destructive actions in the application. The codebase already has a robust `useConfirmation` hook and `ConfirmationDialog` component with variant support (danger/warning/info), async loading states, focus restoration, and dialog queuing. This feature ensures 100% coverage of critical actions with confirmation prompts, standardizes messaging (titles, descriptions, button labels), closes accessibility gaps (ARIA roles, focus trapping, screen reader announcements), migrates the ad-hoc tool deletion modal to the standard dialog system, and adds double-click/rapid-submission guards to all confirmation flows. All changes are frontend-only вЂ” no backend modifications required.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript ~5.9 (frontend), Python 3.13 (backend вЂ” no changes)
**Primary Dependencies**: React 19.2, React Router DOM 7.13, TanStack React Query 5.90, Radix UI (slot, tooltip), Tailwind CSS 4.2, CVA 0.7, Lucide React 0.577
**Storage**: N/A (frontend-only changes; no new persistence)
**Testing**: Vitest with happy-dom, `npm run test` in frontend/
**Target Platform**: Modern web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
**Project Type**: Web application (frontend + backend)
**Performance Goals**: Confirmation prompts render in <50ms; no perceptible delay on action trigger
**Constraints**: All prompts must meet WCAG 2.1 AA; no new runtime dependencies
**Scale/Scope**: ~6 modified frontend files, ~0 new files (infrastructure already exists), ~200вЂ“400 lines changed

**Structure Decision**: Web application structure (frontend + backend). All changes are confined to `frontend/src/` вЂ” specifically the existing confirmation infrastructure (`hooks/useConfirmation.tsx`, `components/ui/confirmation-dialog.tsx`) and the consumer components that trigger critical actions. No new files are created; the existing infrastructure is extended and standardized.
